## [00:00 - 03:02]

So this is an example of building up a for funnel system This particular template is one of the templates we use for e-com So at the top of the funnel you've got your cold audiences that are They haven't clicked your ads they may or may not know about your brand But generally these are people that have not clicked or engaged So we don't have data on them then the next level down is warm So it could be video viewers fans etc etc all the way down to hot For me I class people as hot once have clicked on the site and you have different levels of hot someone That's landed on your home page or blog or product page or cart page They have different values, but generally for me I target them as people that are hot to the visit my site and then existing customers So that's an example of us kind of structuring up an e-com layer and then what we do is to turn that into our Campaign names which will go through in a bit more detail Essentially here is an example of an e-com ad account where we have that system broken out So at the top of the funnel we have our cold audiences and those cold audiences are split between testing and Prospecting now this particular ad account is established. There isn't as much testing going on especially for Audiences because we have mature audiences. We know our Audience really well so you can see some of the examples there We have some lookalikes running some broader than broad campaigns, which is no lookalike no interests We have some interest campaigns some super lookalikes that were running as well and essentially the only test campaign We're running right now is the phase two testing We should just looking at ad testing and that was recently actually turned off. This is a stable ad account now I do Recommend that you're always testing and we are switching tests on and off frequently on this ad account But here's the cool thing once you get stability Your velocity of testing can slow down so really for this ad account because we have stable audiences Which is our phase one of testing we focus more on ad testing. So this particularly com store is appareil So when we kind of come up with new Products ranges launches will be testing different creatives Maybe you'll do some stories testing for Instagram or we do some different ads that we want to play around with your carousel Or different ad formats But they're frequently turned on and off as and when we need them and then we push them into our prospecting campaigns And you can see the levels are Spend for our prospecting campaigns here and I'll break that out shortly Then you see the split from cold audiences to warm so warmer people that have engaged In our fan page They may have engaged with videos for example our Instagram page Facebook page, etc People that are on our email list as well So email list especially people have not clicked would fall into a warm audience as well

It doesn't need as much maintenance For example good spend levels good return on ad spend and has a good active attribution window Which means sales continue to trickle in for another five ten fifteen days for example, and then right at the bottom We have our existing customer campaign. We want people to click but also partly for engagement We want people to engage and then bring them into our world is something which a lot of performance masters don't really Understand is how to best use Facebook to bring people into your funnel It's not always to get the clicks sometimes it's the engagement and you can come back and convert them later as we do And I'll show you the split in revenue then we have our hot campaigns only 12% of our budget is going into our Website retargeting and only 7% of our budget goes into existing customers now You might look at some of these row us numbers and say, you know, you should spend more money there But the fact is these audiences are smaller and we spend as much as we can Without having a negative impact on performance is really important to understand that the lower the funnel you go The fewer the customers and also the potential for your frequency to increase so much that damages performance I'm not one of those that will say, you know, you need to monitor frequency at the bottom of the funnel It's not the case. ## [06:07 - 09:12]

So if you're selling apparel people know what clothes are for example So you don't need to explain the benefits of wearing clothes You do need to explain the benefit of wearing your clothes the clothes that you're selling for example So for us we do that in the cold we bring people in based on Attracting them into our ideal buyer for this particular market We create video ads that show how nice the apparel looks in different situations, etc And some of that drives sales Some of it drives engagement and then really down here We're now starting to work on bringing people in based on user generated content Based on testimonials and things like that So we really want to kind of trickle them through and you can see that in the results that a lot of our performances coming here In terms of revenue and then you look at our Existing customers so only seven percent of our budget so seven k in this particular month Went into existing customers generated 12 percent of total revenue That's I think it's like 4x row ass could be could be better three 3.5 whatever it is But the thing is the important thing is understanding how to trickle your customers through and understanding the difference between What you're spending top of the funnel and where the money is actually coming from so that's an example For e-com and to give you another kind of illustrative example This is a digital product.
